what_it_does: Records a channel attachment metadata entry that links attachment att-42dd609c in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-1ac8af50, with type board_items and an added timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment_type of board_items.
The target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-1ac8af50.
It belongs to the dragnet-development channel.
standouts: empty body with only metadata frontmatter
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external entities with URI-style identifiers (board://...).
concerns: The file has no body or description, so the semantic reason for the attachment is not recorded locally.
Target board item is referenced by URI but no title or context is included.
notes: This is a minimal channel-attachment ledger stub that points a board item to the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or discussion does this attachment represent?
What is the nature of board item item-1ac8af50?
Is the target URI resolvable within this repository?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a board_items attachment matches the content: it records a link to a board item. However, it contains only the reference and no attached content, so it functions as a metadata placeholder.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a discoverable, timestamped record associating a channel attachment with a project board item for later retrieval or audit.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI scheme to target a board item from a channel attachment record.
contribution: Provides a precise, timestamped association between a channel attachment identifier and a board item identifier, which a fused system can use to reconstruct cross-tool links.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata record in the dragnet-development channel that links attachment att-42dd609c to board item item-1ac8af50 as a board_items entry.
intention_for_embedding: To record when and how a channel attachment was attached to a board item for cross-reference and audit.
